mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate capacity utilization and supply-side strength, triggering short-term momentum buying that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of post-shock recovery periods, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance with Rank IC below 0.0 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting higher corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple expansion cycles, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance at p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening, increasing consumer spending power and corporate earnings visibility to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of early-cycle observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, acting as a leading indicator of economic slowdown that forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases with forward returns remaining positive in more than 60% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.01.
